jobs.title: Environmental Geologist
jobs.description:
**Environmental Project Manager**

Giles Engineering Associates, Inc., a leading provider of Environmental, Geotechnical, Subsurface Exploration and Construction Materials Testing services, is looking to expand our Environmental engineering department and is actively seeking Mid and Senior Level Environmental Engineers/Geologists to join our Orange, CA team. All candidates should possess excellent written and verbal communication skills, along with strong organizational capabilities and be able to multi\-task.

Established in 1976, Giles provides a wide range of services to our clients on local, regional and national levels. In addition to our corporate office located in Waukesha, WI, we also maintain branch offices located in Los Angeles, CA and Dallas, TX.

Candidates should have earned a bachelor’s degree in geology, hydrogeology, and/or environmental engineering, with master’s degree preferred. Licensed PG/PE in California desired. Minimum of 3 years of experience since college graduation. Work is based out of Giles’ Orange County California office.

**Responsibilities/Skills**

· Required to perform Phase I \& II Environmental Site Assessments (ESAs) (both field work and preparing reports), Development of Remedial Action Plans, preparation of Closure Documents, and other work as assigned.

· Have and maintain a current Hazwopper Certification. OSHA 40\-Hour Health and Safety Waste Site Worker Training and Annual Refresher

· Responsible for technical execution and completion of multiple concurrent projects

· Preparation of proposals

· Defines the project scope of work, project strategy, project schedule, educates self and client regarding regulatory expectations and requirements, and negotiates outcomes with regulatory agencies as a client advocate.

· Ensures project is executed correctly, on time and on budget. Communicates deviations to the client and client manager to assure compliance with contractual agreements

· Maintains understanding of project financial issues including: terms, changes in proposed scope, and billing process

· Knows and understands applicable environmental guidance documents, codes, regulations and laws, in particular for the State of California

· Monitors project performance

· Implements applicable Giles policies

**Giles offers competitive benefits**

· Competitive Salary

· Health Insurance

· 401(k) and Profit Sharing

· Advancement / Relocation Opportunities

· Educational Opportunities

· Pleasant Work Environment

· Team Atmosphere

Job Type: Full\-time

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* geologist: 3 years (Required)

License/Certification:

* Driver's License (Required)

Location:

* Orange, CA 92865 (Preferred)

Ability to Commute:

* Orange, CA 92865 (Required)

Ability to Relocate:

* Orange, CA 92865: Relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Field Geophysicist / Field Geologist
jobs.description:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!  


At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible. **Job Description:**

**Job Description**:


As a Field Geophysicist / Field Geologist (Associate Scientist), you will support MMRP (Military Munitions Response Program) and HTRW (Hazardous, Toxic, and Radioactive Waste) investigations and remediation projects. Under the supervision of Site Geophysicists, Site Geologists, and Site Managers, you will receive training and gain knowledge of geophysical and geological data collection techniques, knowledge of sampling techniques, and leadership skills in the field. Mastering these skills will allow you to confidently become a Field Team Lead and/or a Site Geophysicist/Geologist on future project sites. You will also support the project management team prior to and after field work by providing technical writing support for work plan development and report preparation, furthering your understanding of the overall environmental process and project progression.

**What you will be doing:**

* Conducting quality control tests and collecting digital geophysical data.
* Acquiring EM61\-MK2, G\-858, UltraTEM, and APEX data.
* Conducting or monitoring daily quality control tests of equipment and methods and documenting test results.
* Completing field work efforts for environmental investigations, including overseeing drilling, conducting low flow groundwater monitoring, push\-ahead groundwater sampling, surface water and sediment sample collection, and soil sample collection.
* Performing basic geologic, hydrogeologic, and hydrochemistry tasks including preparation of cross sections and drilling logs, compiling environmental sample data and conducting analyses.
* Supporting the project team by assisting with technical writing for work plan development and report preparation before and after the field effort.

 **What required skills you will bring:**

* Four\-year degree in geophysics, geology, or related field and 0\+ years experience
* Good written and oral communication skills.
* Experience with Microsoft Suite

**Desired, but not required, skills:**

* Experience with field geophysical data acquisition using EM61\-MK2, G\-858, UltraTEM, or APEX.
* Experience with field geophysical data acquisition using RTK GPS, RTS, or SLAM positioning equipment.
* Performing anomaly reacquisition using EM61\-MK2, and RTK GPS sensors.
* Experience with ArcGIS or QGIS software.
* Experience with Montaj, BT Field Software, and EMClass data processing software.
* Performing field and office tasks requiring the application of basic geophysical principles and theories.
* Experience using MS Access databases.
* Leadership qualities/experience

**Special Job Requirements:**

* Unless already completed, will be required to obtain OSHA 40\-hour HAZWOPER training upon start (at company expense).
* OSHA\-required baseline physical and annual physicals thereafter (at company expense)
* Fleet driver training (at company expense) and a clean driving record.
* Extensive travel to field sites required \- 80% plus travel initially.
* Fieldwork involves performing physically demanding tasks and working 10\-hour days (typically 4 to 5 10\-hour days per week), potentially in adverse field conditions (cold, hot, high wind, dusty environment, rough terrain), and often in remote locations.

**Security Clearance Requirement:**


None
This position is part of our Federal Solutions team.
The Federal Solutions segment delivers resources to our US government customers that ensure the success of missions around the globe. Our intelligent employees drive the state of the art as they provide services and solutions in the areas of defense, security, intelligence, infrastructure, and environmental. We promote a culture of excellence and close\-knit teams that take pride in delivering, protecting, and sustaining our nation's most critical assets, from Earth to cyberspace. Throughout the company, our people are anticipating what’s next to deliver the solutions our customers need now.
Salary Range: $22\.93 \- $40\.14
We value our employees and want our employees to take care of their overall wellbeing, which is why we offer best\-in\-class benefits such as medical, dental, vision, paid time off, Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP), 401(k), life insurance, flexible work schedules, and holidays to fit your busy lifestyle!
This position will be posted for a minimum of 3 days and will continue to be posted for an average of 30 days until a qualified applicant is selected or the position has been cancelled.
Parsons is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, veteran status or any other protected status.
We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent\-recruitment/.
  
COMPETITIVE BENEFIT OFFERINGS  

Financial Wellness  

We care about your financial wellbeing. Parsons offers competitive pay and retirement plans to help you build wealth for the future while giving you the flexibility to diversify your investments.
Work Life Harmony  

Balance in life is important and time away from the office is imperative to allow you to refresh and focus your attention on the things that matter to you. Parsons supports your time away by providing paid time off and pa
